adCopyPlan.benefitBanner: BASIC SALARY + DAILY OVERTIME UP TO 4 HOURS

reasons: Recommended STRUCTURED_PROFESSIONAL for 5 position(s), ~5 headcount, 2 benefit(s), 3 interview event(s).
High-Density scored low: 5 positions / ~5 headcount does not justify a vacancy table.

results.blockReason: Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 90). Defects: The role and country emphasis could be stronger for immediate attention on smaller mobile displays; The blue banner for interview dates slightly reduces headline clarity due to the DOM separation; The QR code looks somewhat separate, integration could be improved visually; The email contact button's color contrast with dark background is low, limiting readability.; Text in the open positions list has alternating row backgrounds that are very subtle and may be hard to distinguish on mobile.; The small green text for numbering in the open positions is of low contrast and may be less legible.; Imagery is low impact and not candidate attracting, though acceptable for structured professional archetype

Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 90). Defects: Text on image partially competing with background due to industrial backdrop but still mostly readable; The agency logo at top-left is quite small, reducing brand identity impact; The email text inside the yellow shape could have stronger contrast for better clarity; Canvas bottom area slightly underutilized with some dead space below the agency details and QR code.; Some text in the body could be spaced slightly better for enhanced clarity; The lower yellow text on green background could benefit from slightly higher contrast

Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 89). Defects: agency logo lacks contrast and is hard to see at the top left corner; contact CTA is not sufficiently prominent or large for mobile social viewing; significant unused white space in the middle and bottom sections results in dead canvas; the key recruitment headline is not visually dominant enough compared to the agency name and location line; the QRC is not visually integrated and appears as an afterthought with minimal styling; IMPROVE_SPACING: The lower half of the image has large empty white space that feels like unused canvas, reducing proportional balance.; INCREASE_HEADLINE_EMPHASIS: 'BILFINGER SHUTDOWN PROJECT' headline could be slightly larger or more visually dominant for immediate clarity on mobile devices.; OTHER: The interview date buttons appear somewhat disconnected and inconsistent in spacing, confusing visual flow.; Repetitive phrasing 'Experience in shutdown projects mandatory' reduces text dynamism; Interview dates could have stronger visual grouping for easier scanning

Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (best score: 88). Defects: Repetitive phrasing in job descriptions reduces scanning efficiency; The job roles block is visually dense and could benefit from improved spacing; The visual hierarchy could be slightly more candidate-first by emphasizing the job roles more than the project title.; The interview dates layout is somewhat tight and could benefit from more spacing for better readability.; Text density is quite high but well organized for newspaper archetype; The QR code looks slightly detached and could integrate better in design; Headline 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' could have slightly more visual emphasis to dominate more
